content_id:
Akibat penekanan berlebihan pada mazhab-mazhab, orang-orang mulai memperlakukan mazhab-mazhab lain sebagai sekte daripada sebagai aliran pemikiran yang valid. Mazhab-mazhab tidak lagi menjadi masalah fikih, melainkan menjadi masalah akidah. Sentimen ini tercermin dalam sistem pengadilan pada masa itu, di mana empat hakim ditugaskan, satu untuk setiap mazhab. Pada akhir tahap ini, situasi semakin memburuk hingga empat azan dan iqamah dikumandangkan, lagi-lagi satu untuk setiap mazhab. Bahkan khutbah Jumat dilakukan empat kali oleh empat imam yang berbeda. Beberapa buku mengambil sikap yang sangat ekstrem terkait mazhab-mazhab hingga membahas pertanyaan seperti apakah seorang pria Hanafi boleh menikah dengan wanita Syafi'i. Jawaban atas pertanyaan ini diturunkan dengan menggunakan qiyas ahl al-kitab yaitu bolehnya pria menikahi wanita dari Ahlul Kitab sehingga boleh bagi pria Hanafi menikahi wanita Syafi'i. Hilangnya Iran ke tangan Syiah juga disebabkan oleh masalah-masalah mazhab. Raja Iran menceraikan istrinya tiga kali dalam satu sesi, yang berdasarkan kesepakatan empat mazhab merupakan talak final sehingga ia tidak boleh menikahinya kembali kecuali jika istrinya menikah dengan pria lain kemudian bercerai.

Namun raja ingin menikahi istrinya kembali. Beberapa menterinya yang beraliran Syiah menyarankan agar raja berkonsultasi dengan imam Jafari. Raja berkonsultasi dengan imam Jafari yang mengatakan bahwa perceraian itu tidak final. Setelah mengetahui hal ini, raja memutuskan untuk berpindah ke Syiah Jafari agar dapat menikahi kembali istrinya. Karena kekuasaannya, ia kemudian memaksa seluruh rakyatnya berpindah ke Syiah. Kekerasan sektarian mazhab pun meletus. Kaum Sunni menentang Syiah dan hampir memusnahkan keberadaan Syiah untuk sementara waktu. Banyak Muslim Sunni melarikan diri ke perbatasan Iran untuk melarikan diri dari konversi paksa dan kekerasan sektarian. Bahkan hingga hari ini, Muslim Sunni di Iran tinggal terutama di dekat perbatasan Iran. Kemudian Hanafi dan Syafi'i mulai berperang satu sama lain hingga Hanafi mengusir Syafi'i dari wilayah tersebut. Kemudian bangsa Mongol menginvasi dan mengambil alih semuanya.
content_en:
Due to the excessive emphasis placed on the madhahib, people began to treat other madhahib as sects rather than valid schools of thought. The Madhahib were no longer an issue of Fiqh, instead they became a matter of ‘aqeedah. This sentiment was reflected in the court systems of the time, where four judges would be assigned – one for each madhab. At the end of this stage, the situation digressed so far that four adhans and iqamahs would be called – again, one for each madhab. Even khutbah al-Jumu’ah would be performed four times by four different imams. Some books took such an extreme stance with regards to the madhahib that they addressed questions such as whether a Hanafi man was able to marry a Shafi’ee woman. The answer to this question was derived using qiyas an ahl al-kitab i.e. it is okay for men to marry from the women of the People of the Book therefore it is okay for a Hanafi man to marry a Shafi’ee woman. The loss of Iran to the Shi’a was due to madhabi issues as well. The King of Iran divorced his wife three times in one session, which by the consensus of the four madhahib was a final divorce, and thus, he would not be allowed to marry her again unless she remarries another man then divorces him. However, the king The Muslim world today Qabeelat Tayybah TCE Notes Revolution 102

wanted to marry his wife again. Some of his ministers who were Shi’a suggested that the king consult the Ja’fari imam. The king consulted the Ja’fari imam who told him that the divorce was not final. After learning this, the king decided to convert to Ja’fari Shi’ism so that he could remarry his wife. Because of his power, he then proceeded to force the rest of the state to convert to Shi’a as well. Madhab sectarian violence thus erupted. The Sunnis were against the Shi’a and they almost exterminated the existence of the Shi’a for some time. Many Sunni Muslims fled towards the borders of Iran to escape the forced conversion and sectarian violence. Even today, the Sunni Muslims of Iran reside mainly towards the Iranian borders. Then Hanafi and Shafi’ee started fighting each other until the Hanafi expelled the Shafi’ee from the region. Then the Mongols invaded and took over all together.
